observations.text: Synthetic 2/1 tearing mode that slows, locks, and ends in a current quench. For UI and contract validation only.

hypothesis: A locked 2/1 tearing mode precedes the disruption.
limitations: This record uses SYNTHETIC educational data only.
This is not FAIR MAST data and is not from any real device.
This record is for read-only analysis and decision support.
This record has no command/control path or hazardous operating procedure.
This record is not a standalone authority for safety-critical operation or reactor design decisions.

context.description: Synthetic post-ignition MHD example: rotating 2/1 tearing mode, mode locking, and disruption, for read-only mode analysis.

observations.text: This context references SYNTHETIC data only.

context.hypothesis: A locked 2/1 tearing mode precedes the disruption.
limitations: This record uses SYNTHETIC educational data only.
This is not FAIR MAST data and is not from any real device.
This record is for read-only analysis and decision support.
This record has no command/control path or hazardous operating procedure.
This record is not a standalone authority for safety-critical operation or reactor design decisions.

shot.notes: Synthetic educational data only. A 2/1 tearing mode that locks and precedes a disruption. Not real measurements.

observationModels.label: Analytic 2/1 tearing mode
assumptions: Constant rotation during the early window.
limitations: Analytic forward model, not a facility simulation.

inferences.label: Phase-fit mode estimate for the Mirnov toroidal array
assumptions: Single dominant rotating mode within the analysis window.
limitations: Toroidal mode number resolved only up to |n| <= 4 (Nyquist for 8 probes).

claims.statement: A rotating 2/1 tearing mode slows, locks, and precedes the current quench (locked-mode disruption).

evidence.rationale: Phase fit across the toroidal array recovers n=1, matching the 2/1 hypothesis (confidence 0.98).
Rotation frequency collapses to ~0 while amplitude grows, then the current quenches: locking precedes disruption.
Post-quench loop-voltage decay is consistent with several scenarios and does not by itself identify the mode.
